operator_instances.transformation_goal: Introduces an optional parameter `include_citation` to control citation inclusion, requiring conditional logic in the function body.
operator_instances.rationale: Increases computation complexity by adding a parameter rule and conditional output, while preserving default behavior.

new_hidden_tests.description: When include_citation=False, the message should not contain citation lines.
new_hidden_tests.call_code:
msg = get_version_message(include_citation=False)
assert "If you use this software please cite" not in msg
assert "macsydata cite" not in msg
assert "Doe et al., 2023" not in msg
result = msg

not_contains: If you use this software please cite

expected_failure_modes: Gets citation lines even when include_citation=False
Ignores the parameter and always includes citation
Function signature does not match the expected parameter

semantic_test_specs.semantic_intent: Test that the optional parameter include_citation correctly controls the inclusion of citation lines.
semantic_test_specs.target_constraint: When include_citation=False, the output must not contain any citation-related strings.
semantic_test_specs.expected_failure_mode: Model's solution always includes citation regardless of parameter, or fails to handle the parameter correctly.
semantic_test_specs.test_case_description: Call get_version_message with include_citation=False and verify that citation lines are absent.

output_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include a test that calls get_version_message(include_citation=False) and checks that citation lines are omitted.
